message.content:
Pizza-making guide for Cheyenne, WY (local context considered)

Direct answer:

You’ll need a practical, quick approach or a classic method. Here are two solid options: a fast no-yeast crust in minutes, or a traditional dough that’s better with a short rise.

Option A — Fast no-yeast crust (20–25 minutes)

Ingredients: 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour, 1 tsp baking powder, ½ tsp salt, ⅔ cup water, 2 tbsp olive oil.

Method:

Preheat oven to 475°F (245°C) and, if you have one, preheat a pizza stone or heavy baking sheet.

In a bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. Stir in water and olive oil until a shaggy dough forms.

Knead briefly on a floured surface to smooth it, about 1–2 minutes. Roll to 1/4-inch thickness.

Add sauce, cheese, and toppings. Bake on a hot stone/sheet 10–12 minutes until crust is golden and cheese bubbly.

Option B — Traditional yeast dough (about 1–2 hours total)

Ingredients (for one 12–14 inch pizza): 2 ½ cups flour, 1 packet (2 ¼ tsp) active dry yeast, 1 tsp sugar, ¾ cup warm water (105–110°F), 1–2 tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp salt.

Method:

Activate yeast: mix warm water, sugar, and yeast; let sit 5–10 minutes until foamy.

Combine flour and salt in a large bowl; add yeast mixture and olive oil. Mix to form a dough.

Knead about 6–8 minutes until smooth. Put in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise 60–90 minutes until doubled.

Punch down, roll to desired shape, add sauce and toppings.

Bake at 475°F (245°C) on a preheated stone or sheet for 12–15 minutes, until crust is golden.

Toppings and sauce basics

Sauce: canned crushed tomatoes or tomato sauce with garlic, oregano, salt, and a pinch of sugar.

Cheese: shredded mozzarella works great; add a little Parmesan for extra flavor.

Toppings: keep it simple to avoid soggy crust—pepperoni, sliced bell peppers, onions, olives, mushrooms, or fresh basil after baking.

Tips for Cheyenne home ovens

Preheat thoroughly; a hot oven yields crisper crusts.

If using a stone, place it in the oven while it preheats for best bottom crust.

Don’t overload with toppings; too much moisture can soften the crust.

If you’re in a rental oven or altitude effects, extend bake time by 2–3 minutes and rotate the pan halfway.

Want a specific recipe tailored to what you have on hand (flour type, toppings, or time available)? I can adjust measurements and steps accordingly.
